macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Global PMIs weaken as stagflation concerns rise
items.summary: Flash May PMI data showed a softer global growth backdrop, with the G4 average composite PMI falling to 49.7 from 51.3 in April, its lowest in 30 months and below the 50 expansion threshold. The readings pointed to weaker demand alongside rising input costs from energy, shipping, and supply disruptions, intensifying stagflation concerns.

items.title: U.S. economy still expanding, but cost pressures are accelerating
items.summary: The May U.S. PMI data suggested growth remains positive but sluggish, while input-price inflation climbed to its highest level since November 2022. The report cited war-related supply disruptions, longer delivery times, and higher energy costs as the main drivers of the deterioration.

items.title: Market now pricing more Fed tightening after stronger inflation expectations
items.summary: The update said one-year inflation expectations rose to 4.8% and long-run expectations increased to 3.9%, above prior ranges seen in 2024. Bloomberg-referenced expectations in the note pointed to a 25 bp hike at the June FOMC meeting, with additional tightening likely later in the year.

items.title: Warsh confirmed as Fed chair in narrow Senate vote
items.summary: Warsh was confirmed by the Senate in a 54-45 vote, described as the slimmest margin ever for a Fed chair. The confirmation highlighted political concern about central bank independence as Powell reportedly plans to remain on the Board of Governors through 2028.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto market sells off as Bitcoin falls below $75,000
items.summary: Cryptocurrency markets experienced a broad downturn, with Bitcoin briefly dropping to around $74,255 and the total market cap losing about $86 billion. Traders faced heavy liquidations, while major altcoins such as Ethereum, Solana, XRP, BNB, and Dogecoin also fell sharply.

items.title: Bitcoin trades in a narrow range after sharp decline
items.summary: Binance reported Bitcoin trading between $74,290 and $77,585 over the prior 24 hours, with BTC last changing hands near $74,688 and down 3.34%. The move reflects continued volatility in the wake of broader risk-off sentiment.

items.title: Bitcoin dips below $77,000 amid ETF outflows and geopolitical tensions
items.summary: A market update noted Bitcoin falling to about $74,666 as investors reacted to Middle East tensions, large ETF withdrawals, and tighter macro conditions. The report also highlighted whale selling and record liquidations across the crypto market.

items.title: Crypto news roundup highlights SEC delay and altcoin weakness
items.summary: A crypto news aggregation page summarized several developments, including reports that the SEC delayed a tokenized stock trading framework, adding pressure to market sentiment. It also surfaced separate coverage of Bitcoin’s drop under $77,000 and other ongoing project and ETF-related updates.

items.title: Bitcoin technical outlook points to further downside pressure
items.summary: A recent market analysis video described Bitcoin as hitting downside liquidity and testing support after a sharp rejection. It also discussed similar weakness across Ethereum and several altcoins, suggesting traders are watching whether support levels hold.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Digg returns as an AI-powered news aggregator
items.summary: Digg is reportedly relaunching as an AI news aggregator that uses X-based signal detection and sentiment analysis to surface stories. The move highlights a broader trend toward AI-assisted news discovery and ranking.

items.title: Google is turning Android into an agentic AI platform
items.summary: Recent reporting says Google is integrating Gemini more deeply across Android, including features like Gemini Intelligence, Googlebook, Android Auto, and widget creation. The effort points to a push toward proactive, assistant-style mobile AI experiences.

items.title: Anthropic roadmap points to proactive AI agents
items.summary: Anthropic’s Claude roadmap reportedly emphasizes AI agents that understand user context and anticipate needs before being asked. The development reflects growing industry competition around autonomous, workflow-aware assistants.

items.title: NVIDIA and Ineffable Intelligence build RL infrastructure
items.summary: NVIDIA and Ineffable Intelligence are reportedly co-developing large-scale reinforcement learning infrastructure for systems that learn from experience and simulation. The work underscores rising investment in training infrastructure for continuously learning AI models.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Bungie reportedly facing significant layoffs amid Destiny concerns
items.summary: Multiple reports say Bungie is planning a significant round of layoffs as Destiny 2 ends, raising fresh questions about the studio’s future and the broader state of its live-service roadmap. The reports also claim Destiny 3 is not currently in active development.

items.title: Xbox reportedly cancels Game Pass family plan due to EA objection
items.summary: A new rumor claims Microsoft abandoned a planned Xbox Game Pass family offering after pushback from EA. If accurate, it would be another notable shift in Microsoft’s subscription strategy.

items.title: Xbox confirms June 2025 Game Pass Wave 1 lineup
items.summary: Microsoft has revealed the first batch of games arriving on Xbox Game Pass in June 2025 ahead of the Xbox Games Showcase. The announcement gives subscribers a look at the month’s early additions.

items.title: Sony State of Play reveals several major game announcements
items.summary: The latest PlayStation State of Play featured multiple reveals, including Team Ninja and Koei Tecmo’s Nioh 3, ArtPlay’s Bloodstained: The Scarlet Engagement, and a new Marvel fighting game from Arc System Works and Sony. The showcase also included other surprise hardware and VR-related announcements.

items.title: Square Enix unveils Final Fantasy Tactics collector’s box
items.summary: Square Enix announced the Final Fantasy Tactics: The Ivalice Chronicles Collector’s Box, though the bundle notably does not include a copy of the game. The reveal quickly drew attention for that omission.

items.title: Game Informer says the first three Resident Evil games and Breath of Fire IV are now on Steam
items.summary: Capcom’s original Resident Evil trilogy and Breath of Fire IV have reportedly become available on Steam, giving PC players new ways to revisit classic titles. The release expands access to several older fan favorites.

items.title: Xbox schedules summer showcase and Gears of War: E-Day Direct for June 7
items.summary: Xbox has set its summer showcase and a dedicated Gears of War: E-Day presentation for June 7. The announcement points to a busy stretch of summer gaming reveals for Microsoft.

items.title: New report says GTA 6 is still on track for November
items.summary: A report highlighted by GameSpot says Grand Theft Auto 6 is not delayed again and remains set for a November launch. The update comes amid ongoing speculation about the game’s release timing.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple says Epic lawsuit should not reshape App Store rules for all developers
items.summary: Apple is pushing back in its dispute with Epic Games, arguing the case should not force broad changes to App Store policies. The move is a significant development in the company’s ongoing regulatory and platform-control battles.

items.title: FBI warns of fast-growing Kali365 phishing-as-a-service targeting Microsoft 365 users
items.summary: The FBI issued a warning about Kali365, a phishing kit being used in recent attacks against Microsoft 365 accounts. Security researchers say the service is helping make phishing campaigns easier to launch at scale.

items.title: Drupal Core SQL injection bug added to CISA KEV after active exploitation
items.summary: A Drupal Core SQL injection vulnerability has been added to CISA’s Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog after signs of active exploitation. Administrators are being urged to patch quickly to reduce risk of compromise.

items.title: Microsoft admits Teams’ interface is too crowded and is working on a fix
items.summary: Microsoft acknowledged user complaints that Teams has become overly cluttered and says it is developing changes to simplify the interface. The update comes as the company continues refining one of its most important business products.

items.title: Anker debuts Soundcore Liberty 5 Pro earbuds with new AI chip
items.summary: Anker introduced the Soundcore Liberty 5 Pro earbuds, part of a broader product push that includes new hardware built around its own chip. The launch highlights continued competition in premium audio and consumer gadget releases.

items.title: Microsoft restructures leadership as it expands cloud and platform focus
items.summary: Microsoft is making management changes as it pushes further into cloud and broader platform growth. The restructuring appears aimed at aligning leadership with the company’s largest strategic priorities.
